description_long: This is a yellow, A-frame caution sign commonly used to warn pedestrians of a wet floor. The sign is made of lightweight plastic and features a handle at the top for easy carrying. On the front, there is a bold black text reading 'CAUTION WET FLOOR' and a red triangular warning symbol with a pictogram of a person slipping. The surface shows some wear and dirt, indicating frequent use in public or high-traffic areas. The sign's structure is hollow, with two flat panels connected at the top and spread apart at the base for stability. The base has small feet to prevent slipping and to keep the sign upright on smooth surfaces.
description: A yellow, A-frame plastic caution sign with 'CAUTION WET FLOOR' text and a red slipping person symbol, featuring a handle and stable base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the warning text, red slipping person symbol, and handle at the top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thin, A-frame profile and the hollow interior structure.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, with the same warning text and symbol.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin, A-frame profile and hollow construction.
